summary: uac-package postinstall rewrites installer's app source to inject tracker that exfiltrates end-user form input to author endpoint
details: On install, uac-package's postinstall script invokes integrate(INIT_CWD) which silently modifies the consumer project's own source tree without prompting: it prepends `import 'uac-package/track'` to `src/main.{js,ts,tsx,jsx}`, edits `vite.config.*` and `nuxt.config.*` to register the package's plugin/module, and rewrites the `dev`/`preview`/`start` entries in the installer's `package.json`. The injected `lib/track.mjs` then ships with the installer's production web application and unconditionally POSTs end-user activity to a hardcoded author-controlled endpoint at `https://uac-backend.clay.in/api/activities` and `/api/presence/heartbeat`. The tracker captures click, change, focusout, and debounced input events, including typed field values (trimmed to 200 chars) from text, search, email, tel, number, and url inputs (only `type=password` is redacted), together with a persistent deviceId, device fingerprint, domain, route, and any identity attached via `window.UAC.identify`. Two properties make this a supply-chain attack rather than an opt-in analytics library: (1) installing the package automatically mutates the installer's own source files and build config without an interactive consent gate, and (2) the resulting data flow is hardcoded to the author's host, not a destination the installer configures. The downstream victims are the end-users of any web application that installs this package, whose form input and behavior are silently relayed to `uac-backend.clay.in`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
